2603.23252.title: AI Lifecycle-Aware Feasibility Framework for Split-RIC Orchestration in NTN O-RAN
2603.23252.paper_summary: Integrating Artificial Intelligence (AI) into Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) is constrained by the joint limits of satellite SWaP and feeder-link capacity, which directly impact O-RAN closed-loop control and model lifecycle management. This paper studies the feasibility of distributing the O-RAN control hierarchy across Ground, LEO, and GEO segments through a Split-RIC architecture. We compare three deployment scenarios: (i) ground-centric control with telemetry streaming, (ii) ground--LEO Split-RIC with on-board inference and store-and-forward learning, and (iii) GEO--LEO multi-layer control enabled by inter-satellite links. For each scenario, we derive closed-form expressions for lifecycle energy and lifecycle latency that account for training-data transfer, model dissemination, and near-real-time inference. Numerical sensitivity analysis over feeder-link conditions, model complexity, and orbital intermittency yields operator-relevant feasibility regions that delineate when on-board inference and non-terrestrial learning loops are physically preferable to terrestrial offloading.
2603.23252.paper_summary_zh: 將人工智慧 (AI) 整合到非地面網路 (NTN) 中受到衛星 SWaP 和供應鏈容量的共同限制，這直接影響到 O-RAN 的閉環控制和模型生命周期管理。本文研究了通過 Split-RIC 架構在地面、LEO 和 GEO 段之間分配 O-RAN 控制層級的可行性。我們比較了三種部署場景：(i) 以地面為中心的控制與遙測串流，(ii) 具有機上推理和儲存轉發學習的地面-LEO Split-RIC，以及 (iii) 由衛星間鏈路支持的 GEO-LEO 多層控制。對於每個場景，我們推導出考慮訓練數據傳輸、模型傳播和近實時推理的生命周期能量和生命周期延遲的封閉形式表達式。對供應鏈條件、模型複雜性和軌道間歇性的數值敏感性分析產生了對運營商相關的可行性區域，這些區域劃定了何時機上推理和非地面學習循環在物理上比地面卸載更可取。
2603.23252.author: Daniele Tarchi et.al.
